Item 1. Business
General
Cisco designs and sells a broad range of technologies that power the Internet. We are integrating our product portfolios across networking, security, collaboration, applications and the cloud to create highly secure, intelligent platforms for our customers' digital businesses. These platforms are designed to help our customers manage more users, devices and things connecting to their networks. This will enable us to provide customers with a highly secure, intelligent platform for their digital business.
We conduct our business globally and manage our business by geography. Our business is organized into the following three geographic segments: Americas; Europe, Middle East, and Africa (EMEA); and Asia Pacific, Japan, and China (APJC).
Our products and technologies are grouped into the following categories: Secure, Agile Networks; Internet for the Future; Collaboration; End-to-End Security; Optimized Application Experiences; and Other Products. In addition to our product offerings, we provide a broad range of service offerings, including technical support services and advanced services. Increasingly, we are delivering our technologies through software and services. Our customers include businesses of all sizes, public institutions, governments, and service providers, including large webscale providers. These customers often look to us as a strategic partner to help them use information technology (IT) to differentiate themselves and drive positive business outcomes.
We were incorporated in California in 1984 and reincorporated in Delaware in 2021. Our headquarters are in San Jose, California. The mailing address of our headquarters is 170 West Tasman Drive, San Jose, California 95134-1706, and our telephone number at that location is (408) 526-4000. Our website is www.cisco.com. Through a link on the Investor Relations section of our website, we make available the following filings as soon as reasonably practicable after they are electronically filed with or furnished to the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) at sec.gov: our Annual Report on Form 10-K, Quarte
…[truncated 19892 chars]…
ross our security portfolio focused on cloud-based offerings, AI-driven threat detection and end-to-end security architectures. Additionally, we continue to invest in expanding our SASE architecture by delivering combined network and security functionality in a single cloud-native service.
5
Table of Contents
Optimized Application Experiences
The Optimized Application Experiences product category consists of our full stack observability and network assurance offerings. Our full stack observability offerings are designed to bring together and provide end-to-end visibility of our customer's environments across applications, networks, multi-cloud infrastructures and the Internet, to help deliver full stack observability for modern environments and drive relevant real-time insights. Our Intersight Infrastructure Services, a cloud-delivered SaaS offering, provides lifecycle operations management, automation, orchestration, and monitoring capabilities for customers' infrastructure deployments from Data Center to the Edge. Our monitoring and analytics offering, AppDynamics, monitors performance across different application-related domains. Our network assurance offering, ThousandEyes, is a network intelligence platform that provides in-depth visibility into network and Internet performance. It enables organizations to see, understand, and improve every digital experience and assure seamless connectivity for their modern digital environments.
Services
In addition to our product offerings, we provide a broad range of service and support options for our customers. Our overall service and support offerings are combined into one organization, Customer Experience, that is responsible for the end-to-end customer experience.
Our support and maintenance services help our customers ensure their products operate efficiently, remain available, and benefit from the most up-to-date system and application software. These services help customers protect their network investments, manage risk, and minimize downtime for systems running mission-critical applications.
We also provide comprehensive advisory services that are focused on responsive, preventive, and consultative support of our technologies for specific networking needs. We are investing in and expanding advisory services in the areas of software, cloud, security, and analytics, which reflects our strategy of selling customer outcomes. We are focused on three priorities: utilizing technology advisory services to drive higher product and services; assessment and migration services providing the tools, expertise and methodologies to enable our customers to migrate to new technology platforms; and providing optimization services aligned with customers' business expectations.
Customers and Markets
Many factors influence the IT, collaboration, and networking requirements of our customers. These include the size of the organization, number and types of technology systems, geographic location, and business applications deployed throughout the customer's network. Our customer base is not limited to any specific industry, geography, or market segment. Our customers primarily operate in the following markets: enterprise, commercial, service provider, and public sector.
Enterprise
Enterprise businesses are large regional, national, or global organizations with multiple locations or branch offices. Many enterprise businesses have unique IT, collaboration, and networking needs within a multivendor environment. We offer service and support packages, financing, and managed network services, primarily through our service provider partners. We sell these products through a network of third-party application and technology vendors and channel partners, as well as selling directly to these customers.
Commercial
The commercial market represents larger, or midmarket and small businesses. We sell to our midmarket customers through a combination of our direct sales force and channel partners. These customers typically require the latest advanced technologies that our enterprise customers demand, but with less complexity. Small businesses require information technologies and communications products that are easy to configure, install, and maintain. We sell to these smaller organizations within the commercial market primarily through channel partners.
Service Providers
Service providers offer data, voice, video, and mobile/wireless services to businesses, governments, utilities, and consumers worldwide. The service provider market includes regional, national, and international wireline carriers, webscale operators as well as Internet, cable, and wireless providers. We also include media, broadcast, and content providers within our service provider market, as the lines in the telecommunications industry continue to blur between traditional network-based, content-based and application-based services. Service providers use a variety of our products and services for their own networks. In addition, many service providers use Cisco data center, virtualization, and collaboration technologies to offer managed or Internet-based services to their business customers. Compared with other customers, service providers are more likely to require network design, deployment, and support services because of the greater scale and higher complexity of their networks, whose requirements are addressed, we believe, by our architectural approach.
6
Table of Contents
Public Sector
The public sector market includes federal, state and local governments, as well as educational institution customers. Many public sector customers have unique IT, collaboration, and networking needs within a multi-vendor environment. We sell to public sector customers through a network of third-party application and technology vendors, channel partners, as well as through direct sales.
Sales Overview
As of the end of fiscal 2023, our worldwide sales and marketing functions consisted of approximately 26,000 employees, including managers, sales representatives, and technical support personnel. We sell our products and services both directly and indirectly through a variety of channels with support from our salesforce. A substantial portion of our products and services is sold indirectly through channel partners, and the remainder is sold through direct sales. Channel partners include systems integrators, service providers, other third-party resellers, and distributors.
Systems integrators and service providers typically sell directly to end users and often provide system installation, technical support, professional services, and other support services in addition to network equipment sales. Systems integrators also typically integrate our products into an overall solution. Some service providers are also systems integrators.
Distributors may hold inventory and sell to systems integrators, service providers, and other third-party resellers. We refer to sales through distributors as our two-tier system of sales to the end customer. Revenue from two-tier distributors is recognized based on a sell-in method. These distributors may be given business terms that allow them to return a limited portion of inventory, receive credits for changes in selling prices, receive certain rebates, and participate in various cooperative marketing programs.

CSCO.quantitative_report:
## CSCO Quantitative Report — Decision Date 2024-07-01

---

### 1. Revenue, Earnings, Margin, Cash-Flow & Balance-Sheet Trajectory

**Revenue trajectory (Q3 FY24 = 2024-04-27):** Quarterly revenue has contracted from a peak of $15.20B (Q4 FY23, 2023-07-29) to $12.70B, a decline of $2.50B or −16.4% over four quarters. The most recent QoQ decline was modest at −0.7% (from $12.79B), but YoY revenue fell −12.8% (from $14.57B in Q3 FY23). TTM revenue stands at $55.36B.

**Earnings trajectory:** Diluted EPS peaked at $0.96 (Q4 FY23) and has since collapsed to $0.46 in the latest quarter, a −52.1% peak-to-trough decline. QoQ EPS fell −29.2% (from $0.65), and YoY fell −41.0% (from $0.78). TTM net income is $12.12B.

**Margin trajectory:** Gross margin has held relatively firm: gross profit was $8.27B on $12.70B revenue (65.1% gross margin) versus $8.43B on $13.59B (62.0%) a year ago. However, operating income collapsed to $2.19B in Q3 FY24 (17.2% operating margin) from $3.95B in Q3 FY23 (27.1% operating margin). Net margin compressed to 14.9% in Q3 FY24 versus 22.0% a year earlier. TTM operating margin is 25.0% ($13.82B/$55.36B), reflecting the sharp recent deterioration masked by stronger earlier quarters.

**Cash flow:** Free cash flow data is unavailable across all reported periods.

**Balance-sheet trajectory:** Total assets jumped to $123.00B in Q3 FY24 from $101.17B in Q2 FY24 — an increase of $21.83B in one quarter, almost certainly driven by the Splunk acquisition. Total debt surged from $7.70B to $20.10B (+$12.40B). Total equity edged down slightly from $46.25B to $45.77B. Over eight quarters, equity has grown from $39.77B to $45.77B (+15.1%), while debt tripled from $8.90B to $20.10B.

---

### 2. Earnings Quality & Balance-Sheet Resilience

**Earnings quality:** The sharp divergence between relatively stable gross profit (QoQ +0.7%) and collapsing operating/net income signals significant operating-cost pressure. Operating income fell from $3.10B to $2.19B (−29.4% QoQ) while gross profit was flat, indicating a substantial increase in SG&A or R&D or acquisition-related costs. The YoY net income decline of −41.3% far outpaces the −12.8% revenue decline, revealing negative operating leverage and/or one-time charges. Without cash-flow data, the cash conversion of reported earnings cannot be assessed — this is an important gap.

**Balance-sheet resilience:** Debt-to-equity is 0.439 (20.10B / 45.77B). While still moderate, it has nearly tripled from 0.202 one quarter prior (7.70B / 46.25B). Total liabilities now stand at $77.23B against $123.00B in assets, implying a 62.8% liability-to-asset ratio, up from 54.3% the prior quarter. The acquisition-driven balance-sheet expansion introduces leverage risk during an earnings downturn.

---

### 3. Valuation vs. Company's Own Supplied History

**P/E:** The current diluted P/E is 15.92×. Over the 12-point sparse history (July 2023–June 2024), the range was 14.58× (Feb 2024) to 20.48× (Sep 2023). The current reading sits at the lower-middle of this range, but notably this P/E is computed against depressed trailing earnings ($0.46 EPS). If earnings remain at this level, the forward P/E on a $1.84 annualized EPS run-rate would imply ~25.8× — materially above the current trailing multiple.

**P/B:** Current P/B is 4.215×, near the bottom of the 12-point range of 4.0–5.6. This is modestly cheap versus history but has compressed alongside the equity expansion and share-price decline.

**Net margin and ROE:** Current net margin (21.9%) and ROE (26.5%) are at the low end of the 12-point history. ROE had ranged from 27.1% to 30.0% over the prior year.

**Earnings yield:** 6.3% (1/15.92) compares to a historical range that bottomed near 4.9% (1/20.48) and peaked near 6.9% (1/14.58).

---

### 4. Price Level & Quarterly/Annual Return Context

**Price level:** $47.51 as of 2024-06-28. The 52-week range is $44.95–$58.19; the stock trades 18.4% below its high and just 5.7% above its low.

**Quarterly return:** −4.8% (from $49.91 on 2024-03-28). The prior quarter was −1.1%, and the one before that was −5.2%. The stock has declined for three consecutive quarters.

**Annual return:** −6.6% (from $50.87 on 2023-06-28). This contrasts with a broader market that has generally appreciated over this period.

**Fundamental-price alignment:** The stock's price decline modestly reflects the earnings deterioration, but trailing P/E has only compressed from ~18.6× to ~15.9× while EPS has fallen from $0.78 to $0.46 (−41%). The multiple contraction has been shallower than the earnings decline, meaning the market has not fully priced the earnings reset — or is assigning some probability of recovery.

---

### 5. Strongest Quantitative Upside & Downside

**Upside drivers (quantitative):**
- Gross margin resilience at 65.1% suggests underlying product/software mix remains favorable; if operating costs normalize post-acquisition, margin recovery could be significant — operating income could recover toward the $3.95–$4.25B quarterly range seen in FY23.
- P/E at 15.92× is at the low end of its one-year range; mean-reversion to the ~18× midpoint, combined with stabilized earnings, implies ~13% multiple expansion upside.
- Equity base has grown ($45.77B vs. $39.77B two years ago), providing balance-sheet cushion.

**Downside drivers (quantitative):**
- Revenue has declined for four consecutive quarters from the $15.20B peak; a continued trajectory toward ~$12B quarterly revenue would push TTM revenue below $50B.
- Operating income of $2.19B is 48.5% below its Q4 FY23 peak of $4.25B; if elevated costs persist post-acquisition, depressed earnings become structural.
- Debt tripled QoQ from $7.70B to $20.10B; further integration costs or revenue shortfalls would pressure leverage metrics.
- Price is just 5.7% above its 52-week low of $44.95; further earnings disappointment could test that floor.

---

### 6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion

CSCO presents a **neutral-to-cautious** quantitative profile. The trailing P/E of 15.92× appears modest in absolute terms and versus its 14.2–20.7 one-year range, but this is against sharply depressed earnings ($0.46 EPS vs. $0.96 peak) and is therefore misleading as a "cheap" signal. Gross margins remain robust at ~65%, but operating leverage has turned sharply negative with operating income falling 29.4% QoQ while revenue was essentially flat. The Splunk-driven balance-sheet expansion has tripled debt and expanded assets by $21.8B in a single quarter, introducing integration and execution risk that cannot be quantified from these numbers alone. Free cash flow data is absent, preventing cash-generation assessment — a material evidence gap.

**Key quantitative tension:** Revenue has declined −12.8% YoY and −16.4% from peak, yet the P/E has compressed only from ~18.6× to 15.9×. If the latest quarterly earnings run-rate ($0.46, or ~$1.84 annualized) is even close to sustainable, the effective forward P/E is ~25.8×, well above the historical range. The stock is therefore cheap on trailing metrics but potentially expensive on forward earnings unless a meaningful recovery materializes. The risk/reward is asymmetric: the downside from further earnings deterioration toward the 52-week low of $44.95 is quantifiable (−5.4%), while the upside requires an earnings recovery whose trajectory is not yet visible in the numbers provided.
CSCO.qualitative_report:
### Qualitative Assessment: Cisco Systems (CSCO)
**Decision Date:** 2024-07-01  
**Evidence Point-in-Time:** ≤ 2024-06-28

---

#### 1. Business/Franchise Read
Based on the 2023 10-K (filed 2023-09-07), Cisco designs and sells a broad range of technologies that power the Internet, focusing on networking, security, collaboration, applications, and cloud. The company is organized geographically (Americas, EMEA, APJC) and manages a shift towards software and subscription-based offerings (e.g., Cisco DNA, Webex). Key strategic pillars include "Secure, Agile Networks," "Internet for the Future" (leveraging Silicon One and optical networking), "End-to-End Security" (with cloud-delivered SASE), "Hybrid Work" (Webex), and "Optimized Application Experiences" (full-stack observability via Intersight, ThousandEyes, AppDynamics). The franchise is characterized by its integrated platform approach, targeting enterprise, commercial, service provider, and public sector customers with a global salesforce and channel partners.

#### 2. Recent Developments (Dated)
*   **Financial Guidance & Performance (2024-05-15/20):** Cisco reported Q3 FY2024 results (period ending Apr 27, 2024) where "revenue, gross margin and non-GAAP EPS... were at the high end or above our guidance range" (2024-05-15). It raised its 2024 revenue outlook to $53.6-$53.8 billion (2024-05-20). The company noted customers are "consuming the equipment shipped over the last couple of years" and benefit from increased spending on AI and cloud computing needs (2024-05-15).
*   **Strategic M&A (2024-05-15):** Cisco completed its acquisition of Splunk, a data infrastructure provider, during the quarter. This had a "positive impact on Cisco's revenue but negatively impacted its earnings" (2024-05-15).
*   **AI & Growth Catalysts Narrative (2024-06-20):** A news article posits that Cisco's investments in AI, humanoid robots, and connected devices could fuel a "Microsoft-like resurgence" (2024-06-20).
*   **Legal/Regulatory Matters:**
    *   **Settlement (2024-05-31):** Cisco reached an $8.6 million settlement for "knowingly selling video surveillance software with critical security vulnerabilities," described as the first successful cybersecurity whistleblower case of its kind (2024-05-31).
    *   **Litigation Outcome (2024-06-13/28):** A U.S. court ruled against the California Civil Rights Department in a caste discrimination lawsuit filed against Cisco, a decision described as a "victory for corporate and Hindu American civil rights" (2024-06-13, 2024-06-28).

#### 3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets
*   **Risk: Security & Compliance.** The $8.6M settlement (2024-05-31) demonstrates potential legal and reputational risk from product security flaws, suggesting possible internal control or oversight issues in software integrity.
*   **Risk: Competitive & Demand Pressure.** The narrative acknowledges "global macroeconomic challenges" and company-specific headwinds that have placed CSCO near 52-week lows (2024-06-19). The need for customers to digest prior equipment shipments (2024-05-15) points to potential cyclical demand softness.
*   **Risk: Execution on Transformation.** The business is in a multi-year transition to software and subscriptions. The Splunk acquisition, while boosting revenue, negatively impacted near-term earnings (2024-05-15), highlighting integration and model transition risks.
*   **Offset: Strategic Tailwinds & Guidance.** Raised guidance (2024-05-20) and management commentary tie growth to secular trends in AI infrastructure, cloud, and 5G deployment (2024-05-15, 2024-06-28). The court ruling in the caste discrimination case (2024-06-28) resolves a specific legal overhang.
*   **Offset: Franchise Resilience.** The core 10-K business description outlines a vast, integrated portfolio and entrenched customer relationships across critical networking and security domains, providing a stable foundation.

#### 4. Concise Qualitative Assessment
**Cautiously Positive.** The supplied evidence shows a company executing on a strategic pivot, bolstered by recent financial results and raised guidance linked to AI/cloud spending. The resolution of a notable lawsuit (2024-06-28) and the narrative around new catalysts (AI, robotics) provide positive qualitative signals. However, this is tempered by confirmed risks related to software security compliance ($8.6M settlement, 2024-05-31), potential demand digestion, and the inherent execution risk of its ongoing business model transformation. The franchise remains large and strategically positioned, but recent developments highlight both opportunity and operational challenges.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Cisco's trailing P/E of 15.9× looks modest but is computed against sharply depressed EPS of $0.46 (down 52% from peak), making the stock deceptively expensive on a forward basis (~25.8× annualized) unless earnings recover meaningfully. Gross margin resilience at 65% and raised FY24 guidance provide a floor, but the Splunk-driven debt tripling and negative operating leverage create material near-term risk that keeps the risk/reward roughly balanced.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: Cisco's trailing P/E of 15.9× looks modest but is computed against sharply depressed EPS of $0.46 (down 52% from peak), making the stock deceptively expensive on a forward basis (~25.8× annualized) unless earnings recover meaningfully. Gross margin resilience at 65% and raised FY24 guidance provide a floor, but the Splunk-driven debt tripling and negative operating leverage create material near-term risk that keeps the risk/reward roughly balanced.
key_factors: Revenue has declined for four consecutive quarters from a $15.20B peak to $12.70B (−16.4%), with YoY revenue down −12.8% in Q3 FY24, reflecting persistent demand digestion headwinds.

Operating income collapsed to $2.19B in Q3 FY24 (17.2% margin) from $3.95B a year earlier (27.1% margin), a −44.6% YoY decline driven by Splunk integration costs and elevated SG&A, despite gross margin holding at 65.1%.

Total debt tripled QoQ from $7.70B to $20.10B following the Splunk acquisition, pushing debt-to-equity from 0.20 to 0.44 and introducing significant integration and execution risk during an earnings downturn.

Management raised FY24 revenue guidance to $53.6–$53.8B (2024-05-20) and cited AI infrastructure and cloud spending as growth catalysts, providing a qualitative offset to the near-term earnings pressure.

## JNJ Quantitative Report — Decision Date 2024-07-01

---

### 1. Revenue, Earnings, Margin, Cash-Flow, and Balance-Sheet Trajectory

**Revenue** is essentially flat: Q1 2024 $21.38B, Q4 2023 $21.39B, Q3 2023 $21.35B, Q2 2023 $21.52B, Q1 2023 $20.89B. YoY growth Q1 2024 is +2.3%; QoQ is −0.1%. TTM revenue is $85.65B.

**Net income** is heavily distorted across the five quarters: Q1 2024 $3.25B (EPS $1.34), Q4 2023 $4.05B ($1.67), **Q3 2023 $26.03B ($10.21)**, Q2 2023 $5.14B ($1.96), Q1 2023 −$68M (−$0.03). TTM net income is $38.48B, almost entirely inflated by the Q3 2023 outlier. Operating income tells a cleaner story: Q2 2023 $6.31B → Q3 2023 $5.22B → Q4 2023 $4.83B → Q1 2024 $3.71B — a steady sequential decline of 41% over four quarters. TTM operating income is $20.06B.

**Gross margin** remains high: Q1 2024 $14.87B / $21.38B = **69.5%**. However, operating margin compressed from 29.3% (Q2 2023) to 17.3% (Q1 2024).

**Cash flow:** No quarterly cash-flow statements are supplied for any of the five quarters. Free-cash-flow and operating-cash-flow figures are unavailable.

**Balance sheet:** Total assets $171.97B (Q1 2024) vs. $195.97B (Q1 2023). Total equity $70.02B. Cash $26.22B, down from $32.32B one year prior (−$6.1B). Q4 2023 total debt was $27.35B; debt for Q1 2024 is not separately supplied. Equity-to-assets ratio: 70.02 / 171.97 = **40.7%**.

---

### 2. Earnings Quality and Balance-Sheet Resilience

**Earnings quality is low on a reported TTM basis.** The Q3 2023 net income of $26.03B versus an operating income of $5.22B signals a ~$20.8B non-operating gain (consistent with the Kenvue spinoff). Excluding that item, a normalized TTM net income estimate is roughly $16–17B, and normalized TTM EPS is approximately $6.50–$7.00 versus reported TTM EPS of ~$15.18. The Q1 2023 loss (−$68M, EPS −$0.03) further underscores quarterly volatility. The operating-income trajectory ($6.31B → $3.71B) is the cleanest earnings proxy and is deteriorating.

**Balance-sheet resilience is adequate but not pristine.** Cash of $26.22B provides a buffer, though it has declined $6.1B year-over-year. The equity base ($70.02B) supports the capital structure; total liabilities of $101.95B imply a liabilities-to-equity ratio of 1.46x. No debt figure is supplied for Q1 2024, limiting precision.

---

### 3. Valuation Versus Supplied History

| Metric | Latest (2024-06-28) | 365-Day Range | Historical Position |
|---|---|---|---|
| P/E diluted | **9.231** | 9.1 – 22.3 | Absolute floor |
| P/B | **5.073** | 5.0 – 6.3 | Near floor |
| P/S | 4.147 | — | — |
| Earnings yield | 10.8% | — | — |
| Net margin (TTM) | 0.449 | 0.2 – 0.4* | Elevated by one-time |
| ROE (TTM) | 0.55 | 0.3 – 0.5* | Inflated |

The P/E of 9.2 sits at the very bottom of its 365-day range. However, **this is an artifact of inflated TTM earnings**. The PE series shows it collapsed from ~20.5x in January 2024 to ~11.4x by February 2024 — exactly when the Q3 2023 one-time gain rolled into the trailing four-quarter window. On a normalized basis (stripping ~$8–9/share of non-recurring gain), the P/E would be approximately **21–23x**, placing it at the **top** of the supplied range. The P/B of 5.07 is near its floor, but the ROE it is being measured against (0.55) is also distorted.

---

### 4. Price Level and Returns Versus Fundamentals

| Period | Close | Δ Price |
|---|---|---|
| 2024-06-28 (latest) | **$146.16** | — |
| 2024-03-28 | $158.19 | −7.6% |
| 2023-12-27 | $156.35 | +1.2% (prior Q) |
| 2023-06-28 | $162.96 | −10.3% (annual) |

52-week range: low $143.13, high $175.97. The stock is **within 2% of its 52-week low** and has lost 10.3% over the past year. This decline has occurred alongside flat-to-declining operating income and a deteriorating operating margin trajectory, so the price action is directionally consistent with weakening fundamentals. The optical P/E compression is misleading because it was driven by the non-recurring gain inflating the denominator, not by price appreciation.

---

### 5. Strongest Quantitative Upside and Downside

**Upside:**
- If the market is pricing normalized earnings (~$6.50 EPS) and assigns the historical median PE of ~20x, the implied value is ~$130, below the current price — so the upside case requires multiple expansion *or* a stabilization/reversal of the operating-income decline.
- Gross margin stability at ~69% suggests pricing power is intact at the top line.
- Cash of $26.22B provides financial flexibility.

**Downside:**
- Operating income has declined four consecutive quarters: $6.31B → $3.71B (−41%). If Q1 2024's $3.71B annualizes, forward operating income is ~$14.8B versus TTM of $20.06B, implying significant further EPS erosion once the one-time gain rolls out of TTM.
- Once Q3 2023 rolls out of the trailing window (around Q4 2024), reported TTM EPS will reset dramatically lower, potentially revealing a normalized PE well above 20x at the current price.
- The stock is at 52-week lows with persistent negative quarterly momentum (−7.6% last quarter, −10.3% annual) and no fundamental catalyst visible in the numbers.

---

### 6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion

The headline P/E of 9.2x is **structurally misleading** — it is an artifact of a ~$21B non-recurring gain in Q3 2023 flowing through TTM earnings. On a normalized basis, the valuation is approximately 21–23x, at the expensive end of the supplied range. Meanwhile, the underlying operating trajectory is deteriorating: operating income fell from $6.31B to $3.71B over four quarters, and revenue growth is negligible at +2.3% YoY. The price decline of 10.3% annually appears commensurate with weakening fundamentals rather than creating a dislocation. With no cash-flow data available, the balance sheet adequate but not compensating for the earnings-quality concerns. **The quantitative risk/reward is unfavorable**: the stock appears to be a value trap where optically cheap multiples mask genuinely deteriorating earnings power and a pending normalization cliff when the one-time gain exits the trailing window.
JNJ.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Assessment Report: Johnson & Johnson (JNJ)**
**Decision Date: 2024-07-01**

**1. Business/Franchise Read**
Johnson & Johnson is a global healthcare conglomerate organized into three segments: **Pharmaceutical**, **MedTech**, and **Consumer Health**. The Pharmaceutical segment is the core growth driver, focused on immunology, oncology, neuroscience, and other therapeutic areas, with key products including STELARA, DARZALEX, and XARELTO. The MedTech segment provides a broad portfolio of medical devices and surgical products. The Consumer Health segment, which includes brands like TYLENOL and LISTERINE, was in the process of being separated into a new public company, Kenvue, as announced in 2021. The company's business model is characterized by a diversified product portfolio, global scale, and a strong focus on R&D and intellectual property protection.

**2. Recent Developments (Dated)**
*   **Corporate Action / Legal Settlement (2024-06-11):** Johnson & Johnson reached a $700 million nationwide settlement with 42 state attorneys general over allegations that it promoted and misled consumers about the safety and purity of its talc powder products. The settlement includes a requirement to cease manufacturing and selling the products.
*   **Narrative / Investor Focus (2024-06-03):** Following a Q1 earnings beat, commentary noted the stock was trading at a valuation of "a little under 14x" and that risks like Stelara patent expiry and macroeconomic factors were considered "already priced in."
*   **Market Perception (2024-06-27 to 2024-06-30):** Multiple financial articles highlighted JNJ as a "dividend stock," "dividend aristocrat," and a stock trading at "intriguing valuations," indicating a prevailing market narrative of the company as a stable, income-generating investment.
*   **Growth Outlook (2024-06-21):** Analysis discussed pathways for JNJ to reach $85 billion in sales, noting its historical sales CAGR of 4.1% (2015-2019) and the strategic importance of its devices, pharma, and consumer businesses.

**3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets**
*   **Risk: Significant Legal and Reputational Liabilities.** The company faces ongoing litigation and reputational damage from its talc products, evidenced by the $700 million state settlement (2024-06-11) and allegations of a "fraudulent" two-step bankruptcy strategy (2024-05-23). These create financial and brand risk.
    *   *Offset:* The company's diversified business model and strong brand portfolio across segments may help mitigate the impact of liabilities in any single area.
*   **Risk: Key Product Patent Expirations.** The 10-K (2023-02-16) explicitly states that STELARA, accounting for ~10.2% of 2022 revenue, has a U.S. composition of matter patent that expired in 2023. This exposes the franchise to biosimilar competition.
    *   *Offset:* The company has a broad pipeline and other growth products (e.g., DARZALEX, TREMFYA) to potentially offset revenue declines. The 10-K notes active lifecycle development programs for many medicines.
*   **Risk: Regulatory and Pricing Pressure.** The 10-K details significant risk from the Inflation Reduction Act (IRA), which authorizes government price setting for certain Medicare drugs starting in 2026/2028 and imposes inflation-based rebates. This could materially impact future pharmaceutical revenue and profitability.
    *   *Offset:* The company's scale, diversified portfolio across segments and geographies, and focus on innovation may provide some resilience against pricing pressures in any single market.
*   **Risk: Competitive and Execution Pressure.** The 10-K states that competition is "particularly significant" in R&D and that the environment requires "substantial investments." The Forbes article (2024-06-03) also cited "challenging macroeconomic factors" and "higher costs" as risks.

**4. Concise Qualitative Assessment**
**Neutral.** Johnson & Johnson possesses a strong, diversified healthcare franchise with leading positions in pharmaceuticals and medical devices. The recent narrative is dominated by its appeal as a stable dividend payer. However, the qualitative outlook is tempered by material and confirmed risks: the resolution of major talc litigation, the imminent revenue impact from Stelara's patent loss, and the long-term threat of government drug pricing reforms under the IRA. These headwinds balance the company's fundamental strengths, leading to a neutral qualitative stance.

With operating income declining 41% over four consecutive quarters, flat revenue growth, and material headwinds from Stelara patent expiry and talc litigation, the risk/reward is balanced at current levels.

Item 1. Business
General Development of Business
Summary Description of Chevron
Chevron Corporation,* a Delaware corporation, manages its investments in subsidiaries and affiliates and provides administrative, financial, management and technology support to U.S. and international subsidiaries that engage in integrated energy and chemicals operations. Upstream operations consist primarily of exploring for, developing, producing and transporting crude oil and natural gas; processing, liquefaction, transportation and regasification associated with liquefied natural gas; transporting crude oil by major international oil export pipelines; transporting, storage and marketing of natural gas; and a gas-to-liquids plant. Downstream operations consist primarily of refining crude oil into petroleum products; marketing of crude oil, refined products, and lubricants; manufacturing and marketing of renewable fuels; transporting crude oil and refined products by pipeline, marine vessel, motor equipment and rail car; and manufacturing and marketing of commodity petrochemicals, plastics for industrial uses and fuel and lubricant additives.

CVX.quantitative_report:
### Quantitative Report: CVX as of 2024-07-01

**1. Revenue, Earnings, Margin, Cash-Flow, and Balance-Sheet Trajectory**  
- **Revenue & Earnings Trajectory:** Quarterly revenue declined from a 2022 Q2 peak of $68.76B to $48.72B in 2024 Q1. Year-over-year, 2024 Q1 revenue fell 4.1% and EPS declined 14.2%. The TTM revenue is $198.87B and net income is $20.36B.  
- **Margins:** Gross margin for 2024 Q1 is 43.1% (calculated: $20.98B / $48.72B). The trailing net margin is 10.2% and operating margin is 14.1%.  
- **Cash Flow:** Data unavailable.  
- **Balance Sheet:** Total assets are stable at $261.65B (2024 Q1). Total equity has fluctuated between $154.56B and $166.25B over the last eight quarters, standing at $161.66B in 2024 Q1. Total liabilities decreased from $103.37B (2022 Q2) to $100.00B (2024 Q1).

**2. Earnings Quality and Balance-Sheet Resilience**  
- **Earnings Quality:** Without cash flow data, cash conversion cannot be assessed. Net margin compression from 14.8% (July 2023) to 10.2% (June 2024) indicates weakening profitability.  
- **Balance-Sheet Resilience:** Debt ratio (liabilities/assets) is 38.2% ($100.00B / $261.65B), down from 40.1% in 2022 Q2. Equity base remains substantial.

**3. Valuation versus Supplied History**  
- **Current Valuation (2024-06-28):** P/E diluted is 14.21, P/B is 1.79, P/S is 1.45, earnings yield is 7.0%.  
- **Historical Context:** Over the past year, the P/E ranged from 8.1 to 15.1, with the current multiple near the top of that range. The P/E has expanded from 8.32 (July 2023) to 14.21. The P/B has been between 1.6 and 2.0, and is currently in the mid-range at 1.79.

**4. Price Level and Return Context**  
- **Price (2024-06-28):** $156.42, within a 52-week range of $139.62 to $171.70.  
- **Returns:** The stock returned -0.8% over the last quarter (from $157.74) and +1.0% over the last year (from $154.92).  
- **Fundamental Path:** While the price has been relatively stable, TTM net income is $20.36B and TTM EPS is approximately $10.87 (sum of last four quarters), indicating a disconnect between the modest price appreciation and the declining earnings trajectory.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside and Downside**  
- **Upside:** Balance sheet strength with a declining debt ratio (38.2%) and stable equity base ($161.66B). The valuation has historically been higher (P/E up to 15.1), suggesting potential multiple expansion if earnings stabilize.  
- **Downside:** Continued deterioration in earnings, as evidenced by the 14.2% YoY EPS decline in Q1 2024 and compressed margins. The current P/E of 14.21 is near the top of its one-year range, leaving limited valuation cushion against further fundamental weakness.

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**  
The stock’s valuation (P/E 14.21) is elevated relative to its recent history and coincides with weakening earnings and margin pressures. The balance sheet remains resilient, but the lack of cash flow data limits full assessment. The modest price return (+1.0% annually) does not reflect the fundamental downtrend. Quantitatively, the risk/reward appears unfavorable, as the multiple is near the upper end of its range while earnings trajectories are negative.
CVX.qualitative_report:
**CVX Qualitative Assessment Report**  
**Decision Date: 2024-07-01**  
**Evidence Window: Up to 2024-06-30**

---

### 1. Business/Franchise Read
From the 2023 10-K (filing 2023-02-23), Chevron is a fully integrated energy company. Its operations span:
- **Upstream:** Exploration, development, production, and transportation of crude oil, natural gas, and LNG. Key geographies include the U.S. (36% of 2022 proved reserves), Australia (16%), and Kazakhstan (14%).
- **Downstream:** Refining crude oil into petroleum products, marketing of fuels/lubricants, and manufacturing of petrochemicals.
- **Strategic Direction:** The company’s stated strategy is to deliver lower-carbon energy while aiming for higher returns and shareholder value. This includes growing traditional oil and gas, lowering carbon intensity, and advancing new energy businesses (renewable fuels, hydrogen, carbon capture).

**Narrative Note:** The 10-K is dated 494 days prior to the decision date, so business fundamentals may have evolved, but no updated annual filing was supplied.

---

### 2. Recent Developments (Dated from Supplied Text)
- **2024-06-30:** Management forecasts boosting production at a >3% CAGR through 2027, driven by the Permian Basin and Gulf of Mexico.
- **2024-06-29 & 2024-06-30:** Chevron highlighted for its dividend yield, described as dramatically higher than its closest U.S. rival, suggesting attractive pricing.
- **2024-06-27:** Mentioned as a key holding in Warren Buffett’s energy portfolio, noted for robust balance sheets.
- **2024-06-28:** Identified as a company positioned to benefit from the U.S. LNG export market.
- **2024-06-26:** Production in the Permian Basin noted as improving amid strong oil prices.

---

### 3. Material Qualitative Risks & Offsets
**Risks:**
- **Regulatory & Litigation Environment (2024-06-28):** The Supreme Court overturned the "Chevron deference" doctrine (as reported 2024-06-28). This could lead to increased legal challenges to federal regulations, creating uncertainty for environmental and operational rules affecting the industry.
- **Competition & Market Dynamics (2024-06-26, 2024-06-29):** The energy sector is described as highly competitive. Recent news notes Chevron as a "laggard" performer relative to some peers, and competitor acquisitions (e.g., Exxon buying Pioneer) may intensify competitive pressures.
- **Economic & Price Sensitivity (from 2023 10-K):** Profitability is highly sensitive to commodity prices, which are influenced by OPEC+ decisions, global demand, and energy transition policies.

**Offsets:**
- **Financial Strength & Shareholder Returns (2024-06-27, 2024-06-14):** Chevron is noted for a robust balance sheet. The 2024-06-14 web context states a three-year median payout ratio of 44%, suggesting a well-covered dividend. Cash returned to shareholders totaled over $26 billion in 2023.
- **Asset Base & Growth Catalysts (2024-06-03, 2024-06-30):** The company holds significant assets in key growth basins (Permian, Gulf of Mexico) and has plans to invest in renewable energy capacity (20 GW by 2025).

---

### 4. Qualitative Assessment
**Positive.**
The evidence supports a view of Chevron as a financially strong, dividend-focused integrated major with a clear growth trajectory in upstream production (Permian/Gulf of Mexico). Its status as a preferred holding for value-oriented investors and its low payout ratio are qualitative strengths. While the regulatory landscape has become more uncertain following the Supreme Court ruling, and competitive pressures exist, the company's scale, balance sheet, and capital return framework provide material offsets. The narrative remains constructive on its core operational franchise and shareholder remuneration.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Chevron's strong balance sheet and attractive dividend yield are offset by declining earnings, margin compression, and a P/E near the top of its one-year range, creating a balanced risk/reward profile.

  note: filing is 521d before decision date; newest available upstream under PIT — a more recent annual 10-K may be missing from the data provider.
Item 1. Business
The Boeing Company, together with its subsidiaries (herein referred to as "Boeing," the "Company," "we," "us," "our"), is one of the world's major aerospace firms.
We are organized based on the products and services we offer. We operate in four reportable segments:
- 
Commercial Airplanes (BCA);
- 
Defense, Space & Security (BDS);
- 
Global Services (BGS);
- 
Boeing Capital (BCC).
Commercial Airplanes Segment
This segment develops, produces and markets commercial jet aircraft principally to the commercial airline industry worldwide. We are a leading producer of commercial aircraft and offer a family of commercial jetliners designed to meet a broad spectrum of global passenger and cargo requirements of airlines. This family of commercial jet aircraft in production includes the 737 narrow-body model and the 767, 777 and 787 wide-body models. We ended production of the 747 wide-body model in 2022. Development continues on the 777X program and the 737-7 and 737-10 derivatives.
Defense, Space & Security Segment
This segment engages in the research, development, production and modification of manned and unmanned military aircraft and weapons systems for strike, surveillance and mobility, including fighter and trainer aircraft; vertical lift, including rotorcraft and tilt-rotor aircraft; and commercial derivative aircraft, including anti-submarine and tanker aircraft. In addition, this segment engages in the research, development, production and modification of the following products and related services: strategic defense and intelligence systems, including strategic missile and defense systems, command, control, communications, computers, intelligence, surveillance and reconnaissance (C4ISR),
…[truncated 8603 chars]…
ion (FAA) and the Department of Homeland Security. Similar government authorities exist in our non-U.S. markets.
Government Contracts.
The U.S. government, and other governments, may terminate any of our government contracts at their convenience, as well as for default based on our failure to meet specified performance requirements. If any of our U.S. government contracts were to be terminated for convenience, we generally would be entitled to receive payment for work completed and allowable termination or cancellation costs. If any of our government contracts were to be terminated for default, generally the U.S. government would pay only for the work that has been accepted and could require us to pay the difference between the original contract price and the cost to re-procure the contract items, net of the work accepted from the original contract. The U.S. government can also hold us liable for damages resulting from the default.
Commercial Aircraft.
In the U.S., our commercial aircraft products are required to comply with FAA regulations governing production and quality systems, airworthiness and installation approvals, repair procedures and continuing operational safety. New aircraft models and new derivative aircraft are required to obtain FAA certification prior to entry into service. Outside the U.S., similar requirements exist for airworthiness, installation and operational approvals. These requirements are generally administered by the national aviation authorities of each country and, in the case of Europe, coordinated by the European Union Aviation Safety Agency.
Environmental.
We are subject to various federal, state, local and non-U.S. laws and regulations relating to environmental protection, including the discharge, treatment, storage, disposal and remediation of hazardous substances and wastes. We could also be affected by laws and regulations relating to climate change, including laws limiting or otherwise related to greenhouse gas emissions. These laws and regulations could lead to increased environmental compliance expenditures, increased energy and raw materials costs and new and/or additional investment in designs and technologies. We continually assess our compliance status and management of environmental matters to ensure our operations are in compliance with all applicable environmental laws and regulations. Investigation, remediation and operation and maintenance costs associated with environmental compliance and management of sites are a normal, recurring part of our operations. These costs often are allowable costs under our contracts with the U.S. government. It is reasonably possible that costs incurred to ensure continued environmental compliance could have a material impact on our results of operations, financial condition or cash flows if additional work requirements or more stringent clean-up standards are imposed by regulators, new areas of soil, air and groundwater contamination are discovered and/or expansions of work scope are prompted by the results of investigations.
For additional information relating to environmental contingencies, see Note 13 to our Consolidated Financial Statements.
Non-U.S. Sales.
Our non-U.S. sales are subject to both U.S. and non-U.S. governmental regulations and procurement policies and practices, including regulations relating to import-export control, tariffs, investment, exchange controls, anti-corruption and repatriation of earnings. Non-U.S. sales are also subject to varying currency, political and economic risks.
Raw Materials, Parts and Subassemblies
We are highly dependent on the availability of essential materials, parts and subassemblies from our suppliers and subcontractors. The most important raw materials required for our aerospace products
4
Table of Contents
are aluminum (sheet, plate, forgings and extrusions), titanium (sheet, plate, forgings and extrusions) and composites (including carbon and boron). Although alternative sources generally exist for these raw materials, qualification of the sources could take a year or more. During 2022, as a result of the Russia Ukraine war, we suspended purchasing titanium from Russia. This has not disrupted our operations as we have been able to use inventory on hand and identify alternative sources. Many major components and product equipment items are procured or subcontracted on a sole-source basis. We continue to work with a small number of sole-source suppliers to ensure continuity of supply for certain items.
Suppliers
We are dependent upon the ability of a large number of U.S. and non-U.S. suppliers and subcontractors to meet performance specifications, quality standards and delivery schedules at our anticipated costs. While we maintain an extensive qualification and performance surveillance system to control risk associated with such reliance on third parties, failure of suppliers or subcontractors to meet commitments could adversely affect production schedules and program/contract profitability, thereby jeopardizing our ability to fulfill commitments to our customers. We are also dependent on the availability of energy sources, such as electricity, at affordable prices.
Seasonality
No material portion of our business is considered to be seasonal.
Executive Officers of the Registrant
See "Item 10. Directors, Executive Officers and Corporate Governance" in Part III.
Other Information
Boeing was originally incorporated in the State of Washington in 1916 and reincorporated in Delaware in 1934. Our principal executive offices are located at 929 Long Bridge Drive, Arlington, Virginia 22202, and our telephone number is (703) 465-3500.
General information about us can be found at www.boeing.com. The information contained on or connected to our website is not incorporated by reference into this Annual Report on Form 10-K and should not be considered part of this or any other report filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC). Our Annual Report on Form 10-K, Quarterly Reports on Form 10-Q and Current Reports on Form 8-K, as well as any amendments to those reports, are available free of charge through our website as soon as reasonably practicable after we file them with, or furnish them to, the SEC. The SEC maintains a website at www.sec.gov that contains reports, proxy statements and other information regarding SEC registrants, including Boeing.
Forward-Looking Statements
This report, as well as our annual report to shareholders, quarterly reports and other filings we make with the SEC, press and earnings releases and other written and oral communications, contain "forward-looking statements" within the meaning of the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995. Words such as "may," "should," "expects," "intends," "projects," "plans," "believes," "estimates," "targets," "anticipates" and similar expressions generally identify these forward-looking statements. Examples of forward-looking statements include statements relating to our future financial condition and operating results, as well as any other statement that does not directly relate to any historical or current fact.

These statements are not guarantees and are subject to risks, uncertainties and changes in circumstances that are difficult to predict. Many factors, including those set forth in the "Risk Factors" section below and other important factors disclosed in this report and from time to time in our other filings with the SEC, could cause actual results to differ materially and adversely from these forward-looking statements. Any forward-looking statement speaks only as of the date on which it is made, and we assume no obligation to update or revise any forward-looking statement whether as a result of new information, future events or otherwise, except as required by law.

**BA Quantitative Report – Decision Date 2024-07-01**

**1. Trajectory: Revenue, Earnings, Margins, Cash Flow, Balance Sheet**
- **Revenue:** Volatile. Latest Q1 2024 revenue = $16.57B. QoQ change = -24.7% (from Q4 2023 $22.02B). YoY change = -7.5% (from Q1 2023 $17.92B). TTM revenue = $76.44B.
- **Earnings:** Persistent losses. Q1 2024 net income = -$355.00M; EPS diluted = -$0.56. TTM net income = -$2.17B. Quarterly EPS was negative in 7 of the last 8 quarters (exception: Q2 2022 EPS = $0.32).
- **Margins:** Weak. Q1 2024 gross margin ≈ 11.3% (gross profit $1.88B / revenue $16.57B). Operating income = -$86.00M. Latest net margin = -0.028 (-2.8%).
- **Cash Flow:** No supplied cash flow data for any period within the lookback.
- **Balance Sheet:** Negative equity. Q1 2024 total equity = -$17.02B. Total debt = $47.69B, down from $56.79B in Q4 2022. Total assets = $134.48B.

**2. Earnings Quality & Balance-Sheet Resilience**
- **Earnings Quality:** Poor. Persistent negative net income and operating income. Gross profit was negative in Q3 2022 (-$822.00M). TTM operating income = -$710.00M.
- **Balance-Sheet Resilience:** Compromised. Negative total equity (-$17.02B) indicates liabilities exceed assets. Debt has decreased ($47.69B vs. $57.01B in Q2 2022), but leverage is extreme relative to negative equity. Cash position is not supplied for recent periods.

**3. Valuation vs. History**
- **Key Ratios:** Latest P/S = 1.459. Net margin = -0.028. Gross margin = 0.1 (10%). Operating margin = -0.009 (-0.9%). P/E, EV/EBIT, FCF yield, and P/B are not available (negative earnings/equity).
- **Historical Net Margin Context:** The supplied point-in-time net margin has improved slightly from -0.06 (July 2023) to -0.028 (June 2024), but remains negative. Valuation is not supported by positive earnings.

**4. Price Level & Returns vs. Fundamentals**
- **Price Level:** 2024-06-28 close = $182.01. 52-week high = $267.54, low = $159.70.
- **Returns:** Annual (252 trading days) return = -13.6% (from $210.72). Quarterly (63 trading days) return = -5.7% (from $192.99). The prior quarter saw a sharp -26.4% decline (from $262.10).
- **Fundamental Context:** Price decline aligns with fundamental weakness: persistent losses, negative equity, and low margins. The stock is trading near the lower end of its 52-week range.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside & Downside**
- **Upside:** Debt reduction trajectory (total debt decreased from $57.01B to $47.69B over 8 quarters). Slight improvement in net margin from -0.06 to -0.028 over the last year.
- **Downside:** Negative equity (-$17.02B) and high debt ($47.69B). Persistent negative earnings and margins. Absence of cash flow data precludes assessment of cash burn or generation.

**6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion**
The quantitative picture is unfavorable. Balance-sheet resilience is critically low due to negative equity and high debt. Earnings quality is poor, with consistent losses and low margins. Valuation (P/S of 1.459) is not cheap given the absence of positive earnings and the negative equity foundation. The stock's price decline (-13.6% annually) reflects the fundamental deterioration. Risk of further fundamental impairment appears elevated absent a reversal in earnings and balance-sheet trends.
BA.qualitative_report:
# Boeing (BA) — Qualitative Assessment
**Decision Date: 2024-07-01**

---

## 1. Business / Franchise Description

Per the 2023-01-27 10-K (period ending 2022-12-31), Boeing is one of the world's major aerospace firms operating in four segments:

- **Commercial Airplanes (BCA):** Produces the 737 narrow-body and 767, 777, 787 wide-body models; developing 777X and 737-7/-10 derivatives. Ended 747 production in 2022.
- **Defense, Space & Security (BDS):** Military aircraft, weapons systems, satellites, C4ISR, cyber solutions.
- **Global Services (BGS):** Lifecycle support—supply chain, maintenance, training, data analytics—for both commercial and defense platforms.
- **Boeing Capital (BCC):** Customer financing and leasing to support product delivery.

Boeing competes with Airbus in commercial aviation and with Lockheed Martin, Northrop Grumman, Raytheon, General Dynamics, and SpaceX in defense. The workforce was ~156,000 as of Dec 2022 with ~50,000 union members; key IAM agreements expire Sept 2024 and July 2025. The company is heavily dependent on sole-source suppliers for major components and on regulated FAA certification for new aircraft.

---

## 2. Recent Developments (May–June 2024)

| Date | Event |
|---|---|
| 2024-06-28 | News articles note BA shares down ~32% YTD 2024, with management needing to address operational headwinds before stock is a "confident buy." |
| 2024-06-28 | Article flags free-cash-flow (FCF) concerns ahead of upcoming earnings; stock rated Hold. Mixed Q1 results reported in April 2024. |
| 2024-06-27 | Boeing awarded a **$115M contract** to procure MH-47G renew-build helicopters for USSOCOM. |
| 2024-06-27 | Article referencing Elon Musk commentary on Boeing's rough start in 2024. |
| 2024-06-25 | Stifel analyst notes Boeing's reported proposition to acquire **Spirit Aerosystems (SPR)** at ~$35/share, viewed as aligning with Boeing's aim to re-integrate its key fuselage supplier. |
| 2024-06-24 | New York Times / Prospect reporting that Boeing may evade criminal charges for violating its 737 Max crash settlement; prosecutors weighing federal monitor vs. lawsuit alternatives. |
| 2024-06-11 | Web context notes "robust demand across portfolio," including orders from American Airlines and Ethiopian Airlines, cited as longer-term catalysts. |
| 2024-05-15 | **DOJ finding** that Boeing violated its $2.5B deferred prosecution agreement related to 737 Max crashes; Boeing faces potential criminal prosecution and a separate **FAA 90-day ultimatum** (issued late Feb 2024) to fix "systemic" quality-control issues, with the deadline expiring ~late May 2024. |

**Summary of capital allocation / corporate actions:** The proposed Spirit Aerosystems acquisition (reported 2024-06-25) signals Boeing's intent to vertically integrate a critical supplier, but terms (~$35/share) and financing details are not yet confirmed.

---

## 3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets

### Risks

- **Criminal prosecution / regulatory escalation (2024-05-15 to 2024-06-24):** The DOJ has found Boeing violated its $2.5B 737 Max crash settlement. Potential outcomes range from a federal monitor to full criminal prosecution. The FAA's separate 90-day quality-control ultimatum (late Feb 2024) compounds regulatory scrutiny. These create material legal, financial, and reputational uncertainty.
- **Systemic quality-control issues (2024-05-15):** The FAA's characterization of quality-control problems as "systemic" signals that fixes may be extensive and could constrain production rates and deliveries across multiple programs.
- **Operational headwinds and negative momentum (2024-06-28):** Shares down 32% YTD with mixed Q1 results and FCF concerns; management has yet to demonstrate a credible turnaround.
- **Union labor risk (10-K, 2023-01-27):** Key IAM agreement expires September 2024—within the forward period—with ~20% of workforce at stake. Negotiations could disrupt production at a sensitive time.
- **Supply-chain concentration (10-K, 2023-01-27):** Dependence on sole-source suppliers for critical components; the proposed Spirit Aerosystems acquisition, if pursued, could be a complex, capital-intensive integration.
- **Competitive pressure:** Airbus remains a formidable commercial rival; SpaceX and major defense primes compete in BDS. Recent news (2024-06-27) highlights Lockheed winning F-35 and satellite contracts that could represent lost opportunity for Boeing.

### Offsets

- **Persistent end-market demand (2024-06-11):** Boeing emphasizes strong order flow (American Airlines, Ethiopian Airlines), supported by secular airline fleet-replacement and fuel-efficiency trends.
- **Diversified portfolio:** Four-segment structure (BCA, BDS, BGS, BCC) and defense backlog provide some revenue stability even if commercial deliveries are constrained.
- **Potential regulatory resolution (2024-06-24):** Reports suggest prosecutors may opt for a federal monitor rather than prosecution, which could limit financial penalties and allow operations to continue with oversight.
- **Spirit Aerosystems re-integration (2024-06-25):** If completed, bringing fuselage production in-house could improve quality control over time, addressing a root cause of production issues.
- **Defense contract wins (2024-06-27):** $115M MH-47G helicopter deal shows continued defense program momentum.

---

## 4. Qualitative Assessment: **Negative**

**Rationale:** Boeing faces a rare convergence of severe regulatory/legal risk (potential criminal prosecution for violating its 737 Max settlement), systemic quality-control scrutiny from the FAA, sharply negative stock momentum (down 32% YTD), and an upcoming union contract expiration—all before confirming a credible path to FCF recovery. While long-term demand for aircraft remains intact and the company is exploring strategic moves (Spirit acquisition), the near-term risk profile is dominated by unresolved legal, regulatory, and operational liabilities. The evidence supports a negative qualitative stance as of the decision date.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Boeing faces a severe convergence of negative fundamentals—persistent losses, negative equity of -$17.02B, and high debt of $47.69B—compounded by acute regulatory/legal risk including potential criminal prosecution for violating its 737 Max settlement and an FAA 90-day quality-control ultimatum. The stock is down 32% YTD with no credible turnaround demonstrated, and the near-term catalyst calendar (IAM contract expiration Sept 2024, DOJ resolution) skews heavily to the downside.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: Boeing faces a severe convergence of negative fundamentals—persistent losses, negative equity of -$17.02B, and high debt of $47.69B—compounded by acute regulatory/legal risk including potential criminal prosecution for violating its 737 Max settlement and an FAA 90-day quality-control ultimatum. The stock is down 32% YTD with no credible turnaround demonstrated, and the near-term catalyst calendar (IAM contract expiration Sept 2024, DOJ resolution) skews heavily to the downside.
key_factors: Negative equity of -$17.02B and total debt of $47.69B as of Q1 2024, with TTM net losses of -$2.17B and negative EPS in 7 of the last 8 quarters, indicating critically impaired balance-sheet resilience.

DOJ finding (2024-05-15) that Boeing violated its $2.5B 737 Max deferred prosecution agreement, with potential criminal prosecution and an FAA 90-day quality-control ultimatum expiring ~late May 2024, creating material legal and operational uncertainty.

Stock down 32% YTD as of 2024-06-28 with annual return of -13.6%, trading near 52-week low of $159.70, reflecting fundamental deterioration and negative momentum with no demonstrated path to FCF recovery.

IAM union contract expiration in September 2024 affecting ~50,000 workers (~20% of workforce) poses additional near-term production disruption risk at a time when Boeing is already under FAA scrutiny for systemic quality-control issues.

The Walt Disney Company, together with its subsidiaries, is a diversified worldwide entertainment company with operations in three segments: Entertainment, Sports and Experiences.
The terms "Company", "we", "our" and "us" are used in this report to refer collectively to the parent company and the subsidiaries through which businesses are conducted.
Human Capital
The Company's key human capital management objectives are to attract, retain and develop the highest quality talent. To support these objectives, the Company's human resources programs are designed to develop talent to prepare them for critical roles and leadership positions for the future; reward and support employees through competitive pay, benefit and perquisite programs; enhance the Company's culture through efforts aimed at making the workplace more engaging and inclusive; acquire talent and facilitate internal talent mobility to create a high-performing, diverse workforce; engage employees as brand ambassadors of the Company's content, products and experiences; and evolve and invest in technology, tools and resources to enable employees at work.

Content Sales/Licensing and Other

TV/VOD Distribution
We license our content to third-party television networks, television stations and other video service providers for distribution to viewers on television or a variety of internet-connected devices, including through other DTC services.
Theatrical Distribution
The Company licenses full-length live-action and animated films to theaters globally. Cumulatively through September 30, 2023, the Company has released approximately 1,100 full-length live-action films and 100 full-length animated films. In the domestic and most major international markets, we generally distribute and market our films directly. In certain international markets our films are distributed by independent companies. In some territories, certain films may be exclusively distributed on our DTC streaming services. During fiscal 2024, we expect to release approximately 15 films, although the ultimate number of releases will depend on when productions resume following the writers/actors' work stoppages.
The Company incurs significant marketing and advertising costs before and throughout the theatrical release of a film in an effort to generate public awareness of the film, to increase the public's intent to view the film and to help generate consumer interest in the subsequent home entertainment and other ancillary markets. These costs are expensed as incurred, which may result in a loss on a film in the theatrical markets, including in periods prior to the theatrical release of the film.
Home Entertainment Distribution
We distribute the Company's film and episodic content in home entertainment markets on DVD and Blu-ray disc, through electronic home video licenses and VOD rentals globally.
Domestically and internationally, we distribute directly to retailers and through independent distribution companies. Electronic formats of our film and episodic content may be purchased through e-tailers such as Apple and Amazon, and MVPDs, such as Comcast and DirecTV, and physical formats are generally sold to retailers, such as Walmart and Target. The Company also operates Disney Movie Club, which sells DVD/Blu-ray discs directly to consumers in the U.S. and Canada.
Distribution of film content in the home entertainment window generally starts within three months after the theatrical release. Electronic formats are typically available approximately four to eight weeks ahead of the physical release. We also license titles to VOD e-tailers concurrent with physical home entertainment distribution.
Distribution of episodic content in the home entertainment window includes electronic sales of season passes that can be purchased prior to, during and after the broadcast season with individual episodes typically available to season pass customers shortly after the initial airing of the show in each territory. Access to individual episodes is also available for electronic purchase shortly after the initial airing in each territory.
Disney Theatrical Group
Disney Theatrical Group develops, produces and licenses live entertainment events on Broadway and around the world. Productions include The Lion King, Frozen, Aladdin and Beauty and the Beast.
Disney Theatrical Group also licenses the Company's IP to Feld Entertainment, the producer of Disney On Ice and Marvel Universe Live!.
Disney Music Group
The Disney Music Group encompasses all aspects of the Company's music commercialization and marketing including: recorded music (Walt Disney Records and Hollywood Records); music publishing; and concerts. Disney Music Group distributes music both physically and digitally and also licenses music throughout the world in various forms of media, including: television; print; gaming; and consumer products.

Linear Networks compete with other networks for carriage by MVPDs. The Company's contractual agreements with MVPDs are renewed or renegotiated from time to time in the ordinary course of business. **Quantitative Report: DIS (as of 2024-07-01)**

**1. Financial Trajectory**
- **Revenue**: Latest Q2 2024 revenue was $22.08B, with a YoY increase of +1.2% (from $21.82B in Q2 2023). TTM revenue (last 4 quarters) was $89.20B.
- **Earnings**: Latest Q2 2024 EPS diluted was -$0.01 (net income $216M), a YoY decline of -101.4% (from $0.69 in Q2 2023). TTM net income was $2.91B.
- **Margins**: Q2 2024 operating margin was 17.4% (operating income $3.85B on revenue $22.08B). TTM operating margin was 16.0% ($14.26B/$89.20B). Net margin was 0.98% in Q2 2024 and 3.26% TTM.
- **Cash Flow**: No cash flow data provided.
- **Balance Sheet**: Total assets declined from $204.07B (Q3 2022) to $195.11B (Q2 2024). Total equity increased from $96.43B to $103.76B over the same period. Total debt was $46.43B in Q4 2023 and $48.37B in Q4 2022.

**2. Earnings Quality & Balance-Sheet Resilience**
- **Earnings Quality**: Volatile quarterly EPS (range: -$0.25 to $1.04 over 8 quarters). Latest quarter showed a large gap between operating income ($3.85B) and net income ($216M), indicating high non-operating charges. Net margin declined from 5.3% (July 2023) to 3.3% (June 2024).
- **Balance-Sheet Resilience**: Equity base grew to $103.76B, with liabilities declining to $91.35B. Debt-to-equity (using Q4 2023 data) was 0.447 ($46.43B/$103.96B). Asset reduction suggests deleveraging.

**3. Valuation vs. History**
- Latest P/E diluted: 62.62 (range over past year: 33.9–77.0). Current P/E is in the upper half of its historical range.
- P/B: 1.755 (range: 1.4–2.1). P/S: 2.041.
- Earnings yield: 1.6% (low). ROE: 2.8% (down from 4.5% in July 2023).
- Valuation is elevated relative to history, but earnings are depressed.

**4. Price Context vs. Fundamentals**
- Latest price (2024-06-28): $99.29. 52-week range: $78.73–$123.74.
- Quarterly return (from 2024-03-28): -18.9%. Annual return (from 2023-06-28): +11.8%.
- Price volatility: rallied +35.4% from Q4 2023 to Q1 2024, then fell -18.9% in the latest quarter, coinciding with the weak Q2 2024 earnings.

**5. Strongest Quantitative Upside & Downside**
- **Upside**: Balance sheet strength (equity growth, debt reduction) could support future investment. If net margins recover toward historical levels (e.g., 5.3%), earnings could improve significantly.
- **Downside**: Persistent earnings volatility and low net margins (3.3%) may pressure valuation. High P/E (62.62) leaves little room for further deterioration.

**6. Provisional Risk/Reward Conclusion**
- The stock trades at a high P/E (62.62) with weak and volatile earnings. Balance sheet resilience is a positive, but declining margins and negative quarterly earnings create uncertainty. Price has already corrected -18.9% in the latest quarter, reflecting fundamental weakness. Risk/reward appears unfavorable given elevated valuation and uncertain earnings recovery.
DIS.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Analysis Report: The Walt Disney Company (DIS)**
**Decision Date: 2024-07-01**

### 1. Business/Franchise Read
Disney is a diversified global entertainment company operating through three segments: Entertainment, Sports, and Experiences. The franchise is built on a vast portfolio of iconic intellectual property (IP) from brands like Disney, Pixar, Marvel, Star Wars, and National Geographic. The business model leverages this IP across multiple distribution windows including theatrical films, linear television networks (e.g., ABC, Disney Channel), direct-to-consumer (DTC) streaming services (Disney+, Hulu, ESPN+), and the Experiences segment (theme parks, cruise lines). The Entertainment segment is undergoing a strategic shift towards streaming, while the Experiences segment benefits from strong brand affinity and global travel demand.

### 2. Recent Developments (Dated)
*   **Content & Theatrical Performance:** The animated film "Inside Out 2" achieved significant commercial success, becoming the highest-grossing film of 2024 and setting records for an animated opening. (2024-06-24, 2024-06-25).
*   **Analyst Sentiment & Market Positioning:** An analyst initiated coverage with a Buy rating and a $125 price target, citing "deep competitive moats" from sports rights and streaming strength. (2024-06-25). Other articles expressed bullish sentiment, highlighting Disney as a top stock pick. (2024-06-29, 2024-06-30).
*   **Streaming Business Progress:** Narrative indicates "considerable progress" and "improved performance" in Disney's streaming business. (2024-06-17). A separate source forecasts an optimistic outlook for earnings across segments. (2024-06-26).
*   **Theme Park Strategy:** Disney World is implementing a revamped premium in-park offering and attraction updates to drive gains. (2024-06-26).
*   **Competitive Environment:** Competitor Netflix is expanding its production infrastructure, indicating ongoing investment in content creation. (2024-06-28). Paramount announced price increases for its streaming service, reflecting industry trends. (2024-06-25).
*   **Legal & Regulatory Proceedings:** Disney must face an antitrust class-action lawsuit concerning its control of ESPN and Hulu. (2024-06-25, 2024-06-26). The company also faces a gender discrimination lawsuit. (2024-06-26). Disney dropped its federal appeal in a Florida lawsuit related to a dispute with the state government, ending that particular legal battle. (2024-06-13).

### 3. Material Qualitative Risks and Offsets
*   **Risk: Ongoing Legal & Regulatory Scrutiny.** Multiple lawsuits (antitrust, discrimination) and the residual effects of past political conflicts create potential financial and reputational liabilities.
    *   *Offset:* The settlement of the Florida lawsuit removes one overhang (2024-06-13). Antitrust suits are common in media and may result in manageable operational changes rather than existential threats.
*   **Risk: Intense Competition & Cord-Cutting.** The linear television business faces secular decline from cord-cutting. The streaming market is highly competitive, with rivals like Netflix continuing to invest heavily in content and infrastructure (2024-06-28).
    *   *Offset:* Disney's strategy bundles linear and streaming assets and leverages unrivaled sports rights (ESPN) and franchise IP to defend its market position. Analysts note these as "deep competitive moats" (2024-06-25).
*   **Risk: Execution in Streaming Transition.** Achieving sustainable profitability in the DTC segment while managing rising content costs and subscriber growth remains a critical challenge.
    *   *Offset:* Recent narrative points to "considerable progress" and improved performance in the streaming business (2024-06-17), suggesting operational improvements are underway.

### 4. Qualitative Assessment
**Cautiously Positive.**

The evidence indicates Disney is benefiting from powerful positive catalysts, notably the record-breaking success of "Inside Out 2" and improving sentiment around its streaming profitability. Analyst support and strategic initiatives in its Experiences segment provide further tailwinds. However, the outlook is tempered by material risks, primarily the burden of ongoing significant antitrust litigation and a persistently competitive media landscape. The balance of fresh, positive narrative around content hits and streaming progress against structural industry challenges and legal risks supports a cautiously positive qualitative stance.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Disney's strong IP franchise and recent theatrical success with 'Inside Out 2' are offset by elevated valuation (P/E 62.62) amid weak, volatile earnings and ongoing legal headwinds. The risk/reward is roughly balanced at current levels.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: Disney's strong IP franchise and recent theatrical success with 'Inside Out 2' are offset by elevated valuation (P/E 62.62) amid weak, volatile earnings and ongoing legal headwinds. The risk/reward is roughly balanced at current levels.
key_factors: Elevated valuation: P/E of 62.62 is in the upper half of its 33.9–77.0 historical range, with earnings yield of only 1.6% and ROE depressed to 2.8% from 4.5% a year prior.
Weak and volatile earnings: Q2 2024 EPS was -$0.01 (YoY decline of -101.4%), with net margin falling to 0.98% in the quarter and 3.3% TTM, reflecting high non-operating charges.
Positive content catalyst: 'Inside Out 2' became the highest-grossing film of 2024, and analysts cite 'deep competitive moats' from sports rights and streaming strength with a $125 price target.

Legal and competitive headwinds: Antitrust class-action lawsuit over ESPN/Hulu control and gender discrimination suit create financial and reputational risk, while Netflix continues aggressive content investment.
