# Goldman Sachs (GS) — Quantitative Report
**Decision Date: 2024-07-01 | Latest Filing: 2024-Q1 (period_end 2024-03-31)**

---

## 1. Trajectory: Revenue, Earnings, Margins, Cash Flow, Balance Sheet

**Revenue** has been volatile but inflected sharply upward in Q1 2024 at **$14.21B**, up **+25.6% QoQ** from $11.32B and **+16.3% YoY** from $12.22B. TTM revenue is **$48.24B**. The prior seven quarters ranged from $10.59B to $12.22B, indicating Q1 2024 is a clear outlier to the upside.

**Earnings** surged in tandem: Q1 2024 EPS diluted **$11.58** versus a trailing range of $3.08–$8.79 over the prior seven quarters. QoQ EPS growth was **+111.3%**; YoY **+31.7%**. TTM net income is **$9.41B**; TTM operating income **$11.98B**.

**Margins** (latest): operating margin **24.8%**, net margin **19.5%**. Net margin has declined from **22.6%** (July 2023) to the current 19.5%, though it has stabilized and ticked up from the 17.2% trough seen in late 2023.

**Cash flow**: No cash-flow data supplied for any period. Free-cash-flow yield and FCF growth are unavailable.

**Balance sheet**: Total assets expanded from **$1,441.80B** (2022-Q4) to **$1,698.44B** (2024-Q1), a **+17.8%** increase. Total equity has been range-bound between **$116.49B** and **$119.29B** over eight quarters, with the latest at **$118.55B**. Equity-to-assets ratio: **7.0%**. Implied leverage (assets/equity): **14.3x**.

---

## 2. Earnings Quality & Balance-Sheet Resilience

**Earnings quality — mixed signals.** The Q1 2024 EPS of $11.58 is nearly double the next-highest quarter ($8.79 in Q1 2023) and almost 4× the weakest quarter ($3.08 in Q2 2023). This extreme quarterly dispersion (coefficient of variation across 8 quarters is very high) is characteristic of trading/investment-banking revenue dependence, making the Q1 run-rate unreliable as a forward indicator. TTM net margin of ~19.5% is below the 22.6% level of a year ago, suggesting that even as absolute earnings grew, profitability per dollar of revenue has compressed.

**Balance-sheet resilience — adequate but highly levered.** Total equity of $118.55B against $1,579.89B in liabilities yields a **7.0% equity ratio** and **14.3× leverage**, consistent with a G-SIB but leaving limited buffer against asset-side shocks. Equity has been essentially flat over eight quarters despite earnings retention, implying capital returns (buybacks/dividends) roughly offsetting retained income. No cash-flow data prevents assessment of internal capital generation.

---

## 3. Valuation vs. Own History

| Metric | Current (2024-06-28) | 1-Yr Range | Position in Range |
|---|---|---|---|
| P/E diluted | **16.31** | 10.4 – 18.9 | 69th percentile |
| P/B | **1.295** | 0.9 – 1.3 | **99th percentile** (at ceiling) |
| P/S | **3.183** | n/a | — |
| Earnings yield | **6.1%** | n/a | — |
| ROE | **7.9%** | 0.067 – 0.09 | Mid-range |
| Net margin | **19.5%** | 0.172 – 0.226 | Mid-range |

**Key observation:** The P/E has expanded **+50%** from 10.87 (July 2023) to 16.31, and P/B has expanded **+33%** from 0.976 to 1.295, over a period when ROE actually *declined* from 9.0% to 7.9% and net margin fell from 22.6% to 19.5%. The re-rating has been driven by price appreciation, not improving return-on-capital metrics. P/B is now at the very top of its 1-year range, leaving essentially no room for further multiple expansion on a price-to-book basis.

---

## 4. Price Level & Return Context vs. Fundamentals

| Period | Price | Return |
|---|---|---|
| 2024-06-28 (latest) | **$452.32** | — |
| 2024-03-28 | $417.69 | **+8.3%** (quarterly) |
| 2023-12-27 | $384.48 | +8.6% |
| 2023-09-27 | $321.95 | +19.4% |
| 2023-06-28 | $313.66 | +2.6% |
| **Annual return** | — | **+44.2%** |

52-week range: **$289.36 – $471.48**; current price is **4.1%** below the high.

**Disconnect:** The **+44.2%** annual price return exceeded the **+31.7%** YoY EPS growth, confirming that the bulk of the return came from multiple expansion rather than fundamental improvement. The four consecutive positive quarterly returns (+2.6%, +19.4%, +8.6%, +8.3%) reflect strong momentum, but the fundamental underpinning (declining ROE and margins) has not kept pace.

---

## 5. Strongest Quantitative Upside & Downside

**Upside:**
- Q1 2024 EPS of **$11.58** represents a potential step-change; if even 60–70% of this level is sustained over the next three quarters, TTM EPS could reach **$35–$40**, implying a forward P/E of only **11.3–12.9×** at the current price — below the bottom of the 1-year P/E range.
- P/E of 16.3× sits below the range ceiling of **18.9×**, leaving ~16% headroom to the top of the P/E range if earnings momentum persists.
- Revenue inflection (+25.6% QoQ) may signal a durable cyclical upturn in capital-markets activity.

**Downside:**
- Earnings volatility is extreme: the weakest quarter in the lookback (Q2 2023, EPS **$3.08**) annualizes to only **$12.32**, which at the current P/E of 16.3× would imply a price of ~$201 — a **–56%** drawdown from current levels. Even a reversion to the 8-quarter median EPS (~$6.60) annualized at $26.40 implies ~$430, roughly **–5%**.
- P/B at **1.295** is at the ceiling of its 1-year range; any earnings disappointment would likely trigger simultaneous multiple compression and earnings revision.
- ROE of **7.9%** is below the cost of equity typically assumed for large banks, limiting the fundamental case for a premium P/B.
- No cash-flow data is available to verify that reported earnings are converting to cash.

---

## 6. Provisional Quantitative Risk/Reward Conclusion

**Balanced-to-unfavorable at current levels.** The stock has re-rated aggressively (P/E +50%, P/B to the top of its range) while underlying return-on-capital metrics have *deteriorated* (ROE 9.0% → 7.9%, net margin 22.6% → 19.5%). The Q1 2024 earnings surge ($11.58 EPS) is a genuine positive inflection, but the extreme quarterly volatility (trailing 8-quarter EPS range: $3.08–$11.58) makes it impossible to assign high confidence to its sustainability. The +44.2% annual return has already captured significant multiple expansion; further upside requires *sustained* earnings at or near the Q1 2024 level — a scenario that, if achieved, would still only bring the forward P/E to the low-to-mid range. Downside risk is asymmetric: a reversion to median quarterly earnings combined with P/B compression from the 1.30 ceiling could produce double-digit percentage declines. The quantitative evidence does not support a favorable risk/reward at the current price and valuation.
GS.qualitative_report:
**Qualitative Report: The Goldman Sachs Group, Inc. (GS)**
**Date of Analysis:** 2024-07-01
**Evidence Window:** Strictly before 2024-07-01

**1. Business/Franchise Read from Narrative**
Goldman Sachs is a leading global financial institution operating through three primary business segments (Filing Date: 2024-02-23):
*   **Global Banking & Markets:** Its core franchise, providing investment banking (advisory & underwriting), FICC and equities intermediation and financing, and market-making. It is described as a critical provider of liquidity and price discovery.
*   **Asset & Wealth Management:** Provides investment management, advisory, and wealth planning services across a broad range of asset classes to institutional and individual clients. It also includes private banking/lending and proprietary investing activities.
*   **Platform Solutions:** Includes consumer platforms (e.g., the credit card program for a major partner) and transaction banking services.

The firm is a bank holding company regulated by the Federal Reserve. Its stated goal is to deliver the full range of its services through its "One Goldman Sachs" initiative.

**2. Dated Recent Developments**
*   **Regulatory & Capital (Stress Test):** On **2024-06-27**, the Federal Reserve announced results from its annual stress test. While all 31 analyzed banks passed, a Bank of America analyst noted GS was the **"worst hit"** among large banks regarding the impact on minimum capital requirements, highlighting the "opacity" of the Fed's process. A JPMorgan analyst described the broader results as a "much bigger surprise than expected."
*   **Legal/Regulatory (Settlement):** On **2024-06-28**, Goldman Sachs, along with JPMorgan, BNP Paribas, and others, agreed to pay a total of **$46 million** to settle an interest-rate swaps case.
*   **Legal/Regulatory (Investigation Request):** In early **June 2024** (2024-06-03), the National Institute for Workers' Rights (NIWR) urged the SEC to investigate possible securities law violations related to Goldman Sachs' diversity practices.
*   **Strategic (Business Streamlining):** The 10-K notes several recent strategic divestitures. In **2023**, the firm completed the sale of its Personal Financial Management (PFM) business and substantially all of its unsecured consumer loan portfolio. In Q4 2023, it entered an agreement to sell its GreenSky business (expected close Q1 2024) and began a process to transition the GM credit card program to another issuer (Filing Date: 2024-02-23).
*   **Market Outlook:** In **mid-June 2024** (2024-06-15/19), Goldman Sachs strategists were noted for raising their year-end S&P 500 forecast to a Wall Street-leading level.

**3. Material Qualitative Risks & Offsets**
*   **Risks:**
    *   **Elevated Regulatory & Litigation Burden:** The stress test results point to potential constraints on capital flexibility. Concurrently, the firm faces ongoing legal settlements (2024-06-28 rate swaps) and is the subject of an SEC investigation request concerning its diversity practices (2024-06-03). The 10-K itself highlights cybersecurity and business continuity as high priorities (Filing Date: 2024-02-23).
    *   **Business Mix Uncertainty:** The firm is actively divesting consumer-related assets (PFM, consumer loans, GreenSky), refocusing but also generating execution risk and exposing the franchise to cyclical investment banking and trading revenues.
    *   **Macro Sensitivity:** The stress test (2024-06-27) and weakening consumer spending data (2024-06-27) underscore the firm's exposure to adverse economic scenarios.
*   **Offsets:**
    *   **Institutional Franchise Strength:** The 10-K describes a leading, diversified global franchise in advisory, underwriting, and market-making, which forms its core earnings engine.
    *   **Passing Regulatory Hurdles:** Despite being "worst hit," the firm **passed** the stringent 2024 Fed stress test (2024-06-27), demonstrating sufficient capital buffers under severe hypothetical stress.
    *   **Strategic Refocusing:** Divestitures of non-core consumer assets (per 10-K) are intended to streamline operations and focus on higher-return institutional businesses.
    *   **Market Positioning:** The firm's public bullish market call (June 2024) and its role in major bank earnings commentary suggest it remains a key voice in capital markets.

**4. Concise Qualitative Assessment**

**Negative.** While Goldman Sachs maintains a premier institutional franchise, the point-in-time evidence leading up to July 1, 2024, highlights a confluence of adverse qualitative developments. The stress test results specifically singled out the firm as the most impacted, indicating potential relative capital constraints. This is compounded by active litigation settlements and a public request for an SEC investigation. Although the firm passed the stress test and is executing a strategy to simplify its business model, the near-term narrative is dominated by regulatory headwinds and reputational challenges, creating a negative qualitative overlay.

pm_decision_obj.executive_summary: Goldman Sachs delivered a standout Q1 2024 with EPS of $11.58 and revenue of $14.21B (+16.3% YoY), but the stock has already re-rated aggressively—P/E up 50% and P/B at the 99th percentile of its range—while ROE and margins have actually deteriorated. The risk/reward is roughly balanced: sustained earnings could justify current levels, but extreme quarterly volatility and regulatory headwinds cap upside.
pm_decision_obj.investment_thesis: Goldman Sachs delivered a standout Q1 2024 with EPS of $11.58 and revenue of $14.21B (+16.3% YoY), but the stock has already re-rated aggressively—P/E up 50% and P/B at the 99th percentile of its range—while ROE and margins have actually deteriorated. The risk/reward is roughly balanced: sustained earnings could justify current levels, but extreme quarterly volatility and regulatory headwinds cap upside.
key_factors: Q1 2024 EPS of $11.58 was a genuine inflection, up +111% QoQ and +31.7% YoY, but extreme trailing 8-quarter EPS volatility ($3.08–$11.58) makes sustainability uncertain and limits conviction in forward estimates.

P/B of 1.295 is at the 99th percentile of its 1-year range and P/E has expanded 50% from 10.87 to 16.31, while ROE declined from 9.0% to 7.9% and net margin fell from 22.6% to 19.5%—multiple expansion has outpaced fundamental improvement.

The 2024-06-27 Fed stress test singled out GS as the 'worst hit' among large banks on minimum capital requirements, and concurrent litigation settlements (2024-06-28 rate swaps) and an SEC investigation request (2024-06-03) add regulatory overhang.

The +44.2% annual price return already captured significant re-rating; further upside requires sustained earnings near Q1 2024 levels, while downside from a reversion to median quarterly EPS (~$6.60) combined with P/B compression from the ceiling could produce double-digit declines.
